subtitle: A 110-metre cove on Bali's Bukit Peninsula reached only by squeezing down a stone staircase through a cleft in the limestone, with one of surfing's most dangerous barrels breaking just offshore.
spike_statement: You reach this 110 m cove by descending ~120 steps through a crack in the cliff; offshore breaks the reef barrel surfers call the Balinese Pipeline.

levers.photo_tone: white sand and turquoise water framed by grey limestone walls, hard tropical light, the dark cleft of the stair entrance
